title: Youth Issues Information Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Crisis Hotlines > Youth Issues Information Services 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BOYS TOWN NATIONAL HOTLINE YOUR LIFE YOUR VOICE HELP SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 24-hour crisis, resource, and referral line. Crisis counselors provide support for issues such as abuse, anger, anxiety, bullying, coping skills, dating and break-ups, depression, eating disorders, grief, parenting and relationship problems, self-esteem, self-injury, school, stress, suicide, and self-care. Offers a mobile app with a mood journal to track feelings in writing or emojis and get tips on how to identify triggers or deal with stressful situations. 14100 Crawford Street Boys Town, NE 68010 Eligibility: At-risk children and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 448-3000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE YOUTHLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis intervention line. Additionally provides support and referrals around such topics as dealing with bad test grades, running away from home, homelessness, a recent breakup, depression, anxiety, bullying, pregnancy, self-harm, or substance use disorders. 5100 SW Macadam Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Youth ages 10-24 and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (877) 968-8491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL SAFE PLACE TXT 4 HELP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential text-for-support service for youth in crisis. This service is intended to assist with issues revolving around abuse, bullying, family problems, depression, and/or suicidal thoughts. Counselors provide support via text messages. 545 South 2nd Street Louisville, KY 40202 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 44357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details STOP IT NOW INC WHAT'S OK? Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential helpline for youth and young adults concerned about their own or a friend's sexual thoughts, feelings, or behaviors. Offers support, guidance, and resources. Eligibility: Ages 14-21 Hours: Monday 9am-5pm, Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-3pm, Thursday 7am-3pm Email (888) 532-0550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINES FOR LIFE YOUTHLINE
